title: Consonants: Retroflex Sounds (टवर्ग)
description: Welcome to the retroflex consonants! These unique sounds are made by curling the tongue backward and touching the roof of the mouth. They don't exist in English, so listen carefully and practice! The five letters are ट, ठ, ड, ढ, and ण.
keyPoints: ट (ṭa), ठ (ṭha), ड (ḍa), ढ (ḍha), ण (ṇa) — the retroflex group
Curl your tongue backward to touch the hard palate to produce these sounds
These are distinct from the dental sounds (त, थ, द, ध, न) — don't confuse them!
ठ and ढ are the aspirated versions of ट and ड

hint: Draw a shape like a hook opening to the left, with a vertical line descending. Add the headline (shirorekha) on top.

text: Which letter is the aspirated retroflex 'd' sound (ḍha)?

memoryTrick: 'Tamaatar' sounds like 'tomato' — they share the same origin!

contextSentenceTranslation: Put the lid on the pot.
memoryTrick: 'Dhakkan' — think 'duck-in' as if ducking under a lid!

hint: Start with a curved shape similar to ट but with the curve opening differently. Add the vertical stroke and the headline on top.

title: Retroflex Sounds — Recap
takeaways: You learned 5 retroflex consonants: ट (ṭa), ठ (ṭha), ड (ḍa), ढ (ḍha), ण (ṇa)
Retroflex sounds require curling the tongue backward — unique to Indian languages
ठ and ढ are the aspirated (breathy) versions of ट and ड
New words: टमाटर (tomato), ठंड (cold), डमरू (drum), ढक्कन (lid)
